title: ARIA Live Region Announcements
description:
Build a **search results** interface that uses ARIA live regions to announce dynamic content changes to screen readers.

### Requirements

- When search results update, announce the count to screen readers using `aria-live="polite"`
- When a loading spinner appears, announce "Loading" using `aria-live="assertive"` with `role="status"`
- When an error occurs, announce it using `role="alert"` (implicitly `aria-live="assertive"`)
- The status announcement must be a visually hidden live region (screen reader only)
- The results count text ("X results found") updates the live region
- A loading state shows a spinner with `aria-busy="true"` on the results container

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Search input | `search-input` |
| Search button | `search-btn` |
| Results container | `results` |
| Result item | `result-{index}` |
| Status region | `status-region` |
| Error region | `error-region` |
| Loading indicator | `loading` |
constraints:
- Live regions must exist in the DOM before content changes (don't mount/unmount them)
- Use aria-live='polite' for routine updates, 'assertive' for urgent updates
- The visually hidden technique must not use display:none
- aria-busy must be set on the results container during loading
hints: Render the live region div (aria-live='polite') on mount with empty text. Then update its textContent when results change — this triggers the announcement.
For the loading state, set aria-busy='true' on the results container so assistive tech waits for loading to finish.
Keep error region in the DOM always; set role='alert' and update its content only when errors occur.
